## reference · Aesthetic-Usability Effect
* The aesthetic-usability effect refers to users' tendency to perceive attractive products as more usable.
* Users are more tolerant of minor usability issues when they find an interface visually appealing.
* A positive emotional response to visual design makes users more patient with minor issues, but not severe ones.
* Form and function should work together; sacrificing functionality for aesthetics leads to user frustration.
* During usability testing, the aesthetic-usability effect can mask UI problems, making them harder to identify.
* To identify instances of the aesthetic-usability effect, pay attention to what users do and how it relates to what they say.
* When users give positive feedback on visual design after struggling with tasks, consider the aesthetic-usability effect.
* To counteract the aesthetic-usability effect during user tests:
  * Reassure participants that their actions are helpful.
  * Use open-ended questions to gather feedback.
  * Distance yourself from the product being tested.
  * Avoid showing emotional reactions to participants' comments.

Sources: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/aesthetic-usability-effect/
